info.title: turnstone Console API
info.description: Cluster-wide visibility and control across all turnstone nodes.

get.description: Returns the complete cluster state: all nodes with their workstreams and overview aggregates. Used for initial load and reconnection.

Server-Sent Events stream for real-time cluster updates. First event is a 'snapshot' with full cluster state, followed by node_joined, node_lost, cluster_state, ws_created, ws_closed, ws_rename events.

get.summary: Redirect to OIDC provider for SSO login
OIDC callback — validates code, provisions user, sets JWT cookie, redirects to app
Return authenticated user info and permissions

delete.summary: Delete a user and their tokens

post.summary: Create an API token (raw token shown once)

get.summary: List channel links for a user

post.summary: Link a channel account to a user

get.summary: List OIDC identities linked to a user

delete.summary: Unlink an OIDC identity (issuer + subject as query params)

get.summary: List run history for a scheduled task
List roles assigned to a user

post.summary: Assign a role to a user

delete.summary: Unassign a role from a user

get.summary: Search external skill registries for available skills

post.summary: Install skill(s) from an external source

get.summary: List version history for a skill

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Start timestamp (ISO8601, defaults to last 7 days)

post.summary: Re-scan a skill for security signals

get.summary: List resource files for a skill

post.summary: Upload a resource file to a skill

get.summary: Get a single skill resource by path

delete.summary: Delete a skill resource by path

get.summary: Get a single memory by ID
List all settings with effective values
Return the full settings registry schema

delete.summary: Reset a setting to its default value

get.summary: Search the MCP Registry for available servers

post.summary: Install an MCP server from the registry

get.summary: List MCP server definitions with live status
Get a single MCP server with status

post.summary: Tell all nodes to re-read MCP server config from DB and reconcile
Import MCP servers from a JSON config file

AuthLoginRequest.description:
POST /v1/api/auth/login request body.

Either username+password or token must be provided.
AuthLoginRequest.title: AuthLoginRequest

jwt.description: JWT session token (if JWT auth is configured)
jwt.title: Jwt

token.description: Raw API token — save this, it cannot be retrieved again
token.title: Token

TokenInfo.description: Token metadata (never includes the hash or raw token).
TokenInfo.title: TokenInfo

node_id.description: Target node: specific ID, 'auto', 'pool', or empty for auto
node_id.title: Node Id

initial_message.description: Optional first message sent after creation
initial_message.title: Initial Message

resume_ws.description: Workstream ID to resume (loads previous conversation)
resume_ws.title: Resume Ws

target_mode.description: auto, pool, all, or specific node_id
target_mode.title: Target Mode

initial_message.description: Message sent to the new workstream
initial_message.title: Initial Message

ScheduleRunInfo.description: Single execution record for a scheduled task.
ScheduleRunInfo.title: ScheduleRunInfo

config.description: JSON config object with mcpServers key
config.title: Config
